sanskrit: यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः । हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः ॥
hindiTranslation: जिससे किसी प्राणीको उद्वेग नहीं होता और जिसको खुद भी किसी प्राणीसे उद्वेग नहीं होता तथा जो हर्ष, अमर्ष ईर्ष्या, भय और उद्वेगसे रहित है, वह मुझे प्रिय है ॥
englishTranslation: The one who disturbs no one and is disturbed by no one, and who is free from delight, envy, fear, and agitation, is dear to me.
essence: True devotion leaves no wake of disturbance.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A beloved devotee does not unsettle others and is not easily unsettled by others. Freedom from excitement, envy, fear, and agitation is part of true devotion.
simpleInsight: There is deep relief in not needing to react, compete, or defend yourself.
detailedMeaning: Krishna is still answering Arjuna on the battlefield, where every quality is tested under pressure. After describing the devotee as steady, humble, and devoted, he adds one more mark: such a person does not disturb others and is not disturbed by them.
modernRelevance: Someone cuts you off in traffic, then flips you off through the window. The old reflex rises fast. This verse points to a steadier way: no inner spike, no need to pass the disturbance onward.
translationLiteral: यस्मात् (from whom) / न उद्विजते (does not become agitated) / लोकः (the world) / लोकात् (from the world) / न उद्विजते (does not become agitated) / च (and) / यः (who) / हर्ष (delight) / अमर्ष (resentment) / भय (fear) / उद्वेगैः (with agitations) / मुक्तः (freed) / यः (who) / सः (he) / च (and) / मे (to me) / प्रियः (dear)
